name: Eggplant Parmesan - Low-Cal
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Combine vinegar, 1 tablespoon olive oil, 1/2 teaspoon each salt and sugar, the oregano, and pepper in a bowl.
2. Coat a large cookie sheet with vegetable cooking spray. Brush both sides of the eggplant with the vinegar mixture and arrange in a single layer on the cookie sheet. Bake for 15 minutes per side or until fork-tender.
3. In a large non-stick skillet, combine onions, garlic, 1 teaspoon oil, and 2 tablespoons wine. Stir over medium-high heat until onions are soft, about 5 minutes.
4. Stir in stewed tomatoes with their liquid, chopped tomato, basil, remaining 2 tablespoons wine, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon sugar. Bring to a boil over high heat; reduce heat and simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally to break up tomatoes, for 10 minutes. This makes 2 1/2 cups of sauce.
5. Spoon one third of the sauce into a shallow 2-quart baking dish. Arrange half the eggplant over the sauce. Spoon half the remaining sauce over the eggplant. Sprinkle with 2/3 cup mozzarella. Top with the remaining eggplant, sauce, and Parmesan.
6. Cover with foil and bake for 30 minutes. Then, remove the foil and bake uncovered for an additional 15 to 20 minutes, or until bubbly. Let stand for 15 minutes before serving for easier cutting. Garnish with basil sprigs.
